updateTitle: EU AI Act — Regulatory Compliance for AI in Pharma

impactedStandards.mappingImpact: New standard mapping required for AI-assisted supply chain decisions
Risk assessment methodology must account for AI-driven quality decisions

impactedControls.controlImpact: New control required for high-risk AI systems in pharma supply chain
impactedControls.requiredAction: Develop AI validation SOP aligned with Article 9
impactedControls.riskIfNotUpdated: Non-compliance with EU AI Act by August 2026

impactedControls.controlImpact: Existing audit trail must capture AI decision rationale
impactedControls.requiredAction: Extend audit log schema to include AI decision metadata
impactedControls.riskIfNotUpdated: Failure to demonstrate human oversight per Article 14

impactedEvidence.requiredUpdate: Create register classifying all AI systems by risk tier
Document human oversight mechanisms for each high-risk AI system

impactedBusinessFunctions.impactSummary: AI-assisted demand forecasting and supplier risk scoring must comply with transparency requirements
requiredActions: Map all AI tools in supply chain
Document human oversight for high-risk systems
impactedBusinessFunctions.riskIfNotCompleted: AI tools may need to be disabled if not compliant by effective date
dependencies: Legal interpretation of 'high-risk' classification for pharma supply chain AI

impactedBusinessFunctions.impactSummary: AI-driven quality inspection and release testing systems require conformity assessment
impactedBusinessFunctions.riskIfNotCompleted: Regulatory action on AI-assisted batch release decisions

impactedBusinessFunctions.impactSummary: Legal review required for AI Act applicability across all EU operations
requiredActions: Review existing AI vendor contracts for compliance obligations
impactedBusinessFunctions.riskIfNotCompleted: Unclear liability allocation for AI system failures

impactedBusinessFunctions.impactSummary: Compliance monitoring program must include AI Act obligations
requiredActions: Add AI Act to regulatory monitoring scope

impactedOwners.action: Complete AI system inventory across supply chain operations
impactedOwners.riskIfNotCompleted: Cannot classify AI risk tiers without complete inventory

impactedOwners.action: Develop AI validation SOP for high-risk systems
impactedOwners.riskIfNotCompleted: No validation framework for AI systems

impactedOwners.action: Update compliance monitoring to include EU AI Act

impactedRisks.riskDescription: AI systems used in supply chain may be classified as high-risk under EU AI Act
impactedRisks.mitigationRequired: Complete AI risk classification and implement conformity assessment

impactedRisks.riskDescription: Insufficient human oversight documentation for AI-driven decisions

recommendedNextSteps: Engage EU AI Act legal specialist
Benchmark against peer pharma AI compliance programs

openQuestions: Does Article 6 classify pharma supply chain forecasting as high-risk?

sourceName: Drug Supply Chain Security Act, Section 582

impactedStandards.mappingImpact: Serialization event reporting must align with updated EPCIS standards
Verification router requirements updated for enhanced verification

impactedControls.controlImpact: Must support enhanced verification with 24-hour response requirement
impactedControls.requiredAction: Upgrade verification system to meet 24-hour SLA
impactedControls.riskIfNotUpdated: FDA enforcement action — inability to distribute product

impactedControls.controlImpact: All trading partners must be verified through FDA-authorized systems
impactedControls.requiredAction: Complete trading partner verification by June 2026
impactedControls.riskIfNotUpdated: Loss of authorized trading partner status

impactedEvidence.evidenceImpact: Existing records must be updated to include enhanced verification data
impactedEvidence.requiredUpdate: Add enhanced verification fields to all trading partner records
Execute IQ/OQ/PQ for upgraded serialization system

impactedBusinessFunctions.impactSummary: Distribution operations must implement enhanced verification at all points of dispensing
requiredActions: Train warehouse staff on enhanced verification

impactedBusinessFunctions.impactSummary: All upstream suppliers must demonstrate DSCSA compliance
Packaging and labeling operations must support updated serialization requirements
impactedBusinessFunctions.riskIfNotCompleted: Non-compliant product cannot enter US distribution

impactedOwners.action: Upgrade serialization verification system to support 24-hour SLA
impactedOwners.riskIfNotCompleted: Cannot meet FDA enhanced verification timeline

impactedOwners.action: Complete trading partner verification for all active partners
Audit top 20 suppliers for DSCSA readiness

impactedRisks.riskDescription: Product distribution halted due to serialization non-compliance
impactedRisks.mitigationRequired: Complete system upgrade and trading partner verification before June 2026

impactedRisks.riskDescription: Supply chain disruption from non-compliant upstream suppliers

recommendedNextSteps: Join industry DSCSA readiness working group
Establish weekly DSCSA compliance war room through June 2026

openQuestions: Will FDA grant enforcement discretion for good-faith compliance efforts?

sourceName: ICH Q12 Technical and Regulatory Considerations

updateTitle: EU GMP Annex 1 — Contamination Control Strategy Requirements
